que: What annotation marks a composable function?
What do composable functions primarily do?
options: Describe UI based on input data

que: What does Android recommend composables should generally be?
Do composable functions usually return Android View objects?
How does Compose generally update UI?
options: By walking the view tree and mutating widgets manually only

que: What is a common composable mistake?
options: Writing side effects directly in the composable body

que: Why is declarative UI useful in Compose?
options: It reduces manual UI mutation complexity

que: What should happen when app state changes?
options: Composable functions can be called again with new data
You must recreate all Kotlin files

que: What is a good composable design practice?
options: Put all business logic in a single giant composable
Depend on execution order side effects

que: Which official docs best support this topic?
options: Compose tutorial and Thinking in Compose
